category_title: Students distracted by personal circumstances and engaging in off-task talk during math instruction
minimal_context: The student and tutor are working through a worksheet on everyday numeracy applications displayed on an overhead projector.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's look at the first question on the overhead projector about calculating the weekly grocery budget.

STUDENT: Why do we even have to do this? I find this curriculum irrelevant.

TUTOR: Please just try to write down the answer on your worksheet.

STUDENT: I can't do it, I'm just not on the planet today. My whole experience of schooling is negative.

TUTOR: We need to keep going with the lesson.

STUDENT: (Calls out to another student across the room) Hey, did you see what happened this morning? I'm not doing this math, I'm not well, cross and cranky today. There's too much talking anyway!

options.A: Keep a kit comprising assorted coloring sheets and art materials to help engage the student.
options.B: Draw the student out of the classroom to talk with them alone about the lesson.
options.C: Prompt the use of a more efficient related math fact to calculate the grocery budget.
options.D: Respond by saying, 'Another question already? No way! I’m so proud of you! Keep going.'
